title: Glucose Tolerance Test with Insulin Secretion in Mice
author: Jason Kim
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.xxbfpin

steps.title: Mice may be fasted overnight (~15...
steps.instruction: Mice may be fasted overnight (~15 hours) or for 5 hours prior to the start of experiment.

steps.title: Collect plasma sample (20 l) before...
steps.instruction: Collect plasma sample (20 l) before the start of experiment (basal-0 min) to measure basal glucose and insulin levels.

steps.title: Administer intraperitoneal injection of 20% dextrose...
steps.instruction: Administer intraperitoneal injection of 20% dextrose (1 or 2 g/kg body weight) using an insulin syringe.

steps.title: Collect plasma samples (20 l) at...
steps.instruction: Collect plasma samples (20 l) at 10, 20, 30, 60, 90, and 120 min following injection to measure circulating glucose and insulin concentrations.

steps.title: For data analysis, plasma glucose levels vs
steps.instruction: For data analysis, plasma glucose levels vs. time after injection are plotted, and areaunder-curve may be calculated to estimate insulin sensitivity. Also, plasma insulin levels vs. time after injection are plotted, and area-under-curve may be calculated to estimate insulin secretion (pancreatic -cell function).
